desc:
Directly overhead, a massive slab of granite forms a natural bridge over this
rushing water, connecting the cliffs on either side of the river. The bridge is
at least fifteen metres above the water, but it may be accessible due to a small
ledge on the western cliff.
name: On a River

desc:
The water here is shallow, but still swift enough to be dangerous, and its icy
touch numbs the flesh almost instantly. The source of this stream must not be
too much farther upstream -- the water is crystal clear. There is a trail
running along the northern bank of the river, but it is impossible to reach due
to the jagged rocks and swift currents.
name: On a River

desc:
Here the river widens slightly, slowing down the strong flow of water. Sheer
walls of stone block any attempt to get out of the river, forcing you to choose
to follow the water west, or to try and follow it east. Perhaps you will soon be
able to leave the water, and continue your journey...
name: River

desc:
With a persistence beyond the powers of Morgoth, the water continues its journey
westwards. The flume through which the water flows here is cut deep into the
mountains, cutting off passage to the south. However, you can climb north on to
a worn path running through the mountains. To the east, you can follow the river
upstream up to a waterfall farther along.
name: River

desc:
The river here is thin and shallow. Although very cold, the water appears very
muddy. Almost red in hue, it flows towards the west. To the east, you see a
waterfall. South of here, you see nothing but mountains. Perhaps, the trail
north of here leads someplace interesting...
name: River

desc:
The trail forks here, with one branch heading east and following the river to
the south, while the other heads north deeper into the mountains. To the west,
the trail crosses the river gorge via a natural bridge, formed by a single huge
block of granite that, when shrugged off the side of the mountain, somehow
landed to form a bridge.
name: Near a Fork

desc:
The river flows just to the south, about 10 metres below the level of this
trail. Either the trail slopes down to the east, or the riverbed slopes up-
ward, for the two seem to be much closer together to the east. West of this
point there is a fork in the trail.
name: Trail along a River

desc:
A daunting cliff looms ominously to the north, dwarfing the small trail that
winds along between its foot and the river to the south. The trail is wet with
spray from the turbulent river, and often the footing is treacherous. To the
east, the trail gets much closer to the river's edge -- perhaps it would be
possible to enter the water there.
name: Under the Cliff

desc:
To the east, the stream becomes more narrow as its source nears. The trail
follows the northern bank, nestled between the icy water and a bleak cliff to
the north. The cold peaks of the Misty Mountains rear up to the east - a
seemingly impassable obstacle.
name: Trail along a River

desc:
This trail seems to go forever, with no end in sight. With the exception of the
river flowing south of you, all you see is stone. Mountains and mountains of
stone... Perhaps some ancient secret can be found in those mountains, but unless
you have some means to cross the river, you'll have to travel farther until you
find a break from the high walls of stone preventing you from leaving this
trail.
name: Trail along a River

desc:
More and more in your journey, you begin to wish you never left home. Although a
rock face might change, or a river might flow, the scenery is as bleak as could
be. On this section of the path, you are fortunate enough to have the dreariness
broken by the sound of a river to the south. Also, you hear a loud splashing
noise to the southeast. Perhaps some vile creature of these mountains is playing
in the water?
name: Trail along a River

desc:
Except for the loud roaring of the waterfall south of here, the mountains seem
quiet and lifeless. Even the trail you're standing on seems devoid of life. Only
the hardiest creatures could survive in this land for long, and you sense that
you would prefer not to have the misfortune of meeting any of them. East and
west, this trail continues, seeming to go deeper into the mountains in both
directions.
name: Trail along a River

desc:
Passing through the lower mountains to the west, the trail follows the side of
the mountain, just east of here, directly to the top. Glancing south, you notice
what is perhaps a mirage of sorts. How could a pool form in these harsh
mountains? Nevertheless, you are drawn to it, even if just to quench your
thirst, and fill your skins. After all, this might be the last chance in these
harsh, lifeless mountains.
name: Trail through the Mountains

desc:
The High Pass turns south here, dipping down into the valley. To the north, you
see a small, windy trail breaking off. The ground looks like it flattens out
some to the north, but you can make out several peaks in the distance. There is
a sign here, carved into one of the larger boulders.
name: Junction

desc:
You continue to walk along the well-defined pass. Known by some as the High
Pass, it is one of the primary trails for crossing these mountains. Though easy
to walk along here, the land right off the path is quite dangerous. You realise
you have to face the dangers of not just the terrain but whatever fell creatures
reside here.
name: High Pass

desc:
Water falling from above splashes you, cleaning some of the dirt caked on your
body. Dripping from your clothes, the water (and dirt) flows west, down through
the mountains. A smile comes to your face when you consider just how far your
dirt will travel before coming to rest at the bottom of the sea.
name: Bottom of a Waterfall

desc:
So different from the rest of the scenery here, a large pool of water stands in
the middle of this landing. It seems to be fed from underground, perhaps from
somewhere beyond the sheer face of rock to the east... The water drains directly
down in a thin, but quickly flowing, waterfall. The falling water flows west to
form a thin river through the mountains. There appears to be a trail of sorts
directly north of here, and to the east you spy a small camp.
name: Source of the River

desc:
The river to the north must have dug this alcove in the side of the mountains.
The only exit appears to be back to the river that you came from. However, this
alcove does seem to offer protection from the creatures of the mountains.
Perhaps this would be a good place to rest for a while.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
As you walk through the valley, you trip on a pile of rocks on the rocky ground.
Looking around, you can see that this debris must have fallen from the mountain
tops east of you. A rock slide must have occurred recently. Hopefully, this
valley will be spared another while you are here. The valley begins to slope
upwards south of here, while going down towards the north where you think you
hear the sounds of splashing water.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
You find yourself inside a U-shaped valley in the mountains. North of here, a
thin river flows westwards, but there is no way to pass it without a boat of
some sort. South, the valley tends to gain altitude. If you are unable to pass
the river, perhaps your destiny lies to the south.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
The climb up the side of the mountain to the peak was more difficult than you
thought at first. Luckily, you made it safely to the top. The humidity of the
air here is quite high, and your vision is blurred in the mist. Looking back
down the direction you came, the mountainside is littered with rubble.
name: Mountain Peak

desc:
Rising high from the ground below, the mountains tower over the land. Higher
still, the peaks of the mountains look down upon the terrain with a view of all
that can be seen. Unfortunately, there isn't much. Just north, you can see a
large pool of water that falls into a river. East of that pool, a single peak
rises high above the rest. In all other directions, you see nothing but the cold
stone of the Misty Mountains.
name: Mountain Peak

desc:
Continuing up the slope, you find yourself near two mountain peaks rising above
you. The southern one is inaccessible, but the one to the east is a fairly easy
climb. Your mind ponders for a moment what might lie on the other side of that
peak. Of course, there is only one way to find out.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
High on the top of this mountain, the lack of air causes you to become dizzy.
You quickly lose your sense of direction for a brief moment, before becoming
accustomed to the thinness of air. You shudder when you look south, as several
of the peaks there are even higher. Perhaps you'll be able to avoid them in your
journey...
name: Mountain Peak

desc:
As you look around at the vast reaches of grey mountains which surround you on
all sides, and contemplate the sameness of it all, you can't help but wonder if
you haven't been in this very spot already.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
High on the top of this mountain, the lack of air causes you to become dizzy.
You quickly lose your sense of direction for a brief moment, before becoming
accustomed to the thinness of air. You shudder when you look south, as several
of the peaks there are even higher. Perhaps you'll be able to avoid them in your
journey...
name: Mountain Peak

desc:
As you look around at the vast reaches of grey mountains which surround you on
all sides and contemplate the sameness of it all, you can't help but wonder if
you haven't been in this very spot already.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
You wonder what force could have dug this alcove in the side of the mountains.
The only exit appears to be back to the east where you came from. Leaving might
be a good idea, as you are easy prey for anything that happens to wander in
here, and it seems it would be too easy to trap you in here.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Both north and west of you, the mountains come to peaks. You can also see a wall
of peaks going from the southwest to the northeast. Although you can climb up
the western peak, the one to the north offers nothing but a vertical wall to its
top. Perhaps it can be accessed from another side. A small cave-like alcove is
south of you.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
You find yourself on a slope that rises higher into the mountains, coming to a
peak above you. The slope flows south, falling into a lower part of the
mountains. Sharp overhangs from the north and east taunt you. Passage in those
directions is impossible.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Taking advantage of the sloping lands, a small camp has been erected here. Using
the rising cusps as walls, the roof has been made of thatched branches and
pelts. Inside, a small fireplace stands cold, a few bits of broken bones stand
testament for its use. Off to one corner, a lantern glows dimly, giving enough
light to illuminate the surroundings.
name: A Crude Camp

desc:
Winding along the side of the mountains, the trail can be followed to a peak
just north of here. As well, there is a very high, snowcapped peak towering
above you. You notice signs of the trail also going south and east of here.
Unfortunately, the blandness of the Misty Mountains hides from your eyes the
true path.
name: Trail over a Mountain Top

desc:
You are standing atop one of the lower peaks of the Misty Mountains. At this
height, you can better see much of your surroundings. To the west, a trail seems
to be following alongside a river. Another part of that trail seems to go
southeast of here. Due east, more of the strong mountains of this region rise
out of the ground. To the southwest, there is another peak higher than this one.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
Winding along the side of the mountain, the trail can be followed to the peak
just east of here. However, to the west you can see a trail that flows next to a
river far below. Although near a peak to the east, south of you is a sheer wall
towards yet another, higher peak of the Misty Mountains.
name: Trail over a Mountain Top

desc:
If you've become tired of wandering aimlessly through the mountains, perhaps you
are in luck. East of here you notice a trail made in this treacherous quarry
called the Misty Mountains. Of course, if exploration is on your mind, follow
the slope down to the south. Perhaps you can find some lost treasure deep within
the mountains.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
You find yourself wandering in a deep valley. All around you, the hard, grey
stone walls of the mountains look down upon you. Both to your east and west you
see the high peaks of mountains. Above you, you notice a shelf that you can
reach. North and west, the valley slopes upwards, rising towards the high
mountain tops.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Fear strikes you as you recall horror stories of large stone giants that wander
deep in these mountains. Sloping upwards from the east, the mountains continue
north and west of here. From your location low in the mountains, not much is
visible here except for the endless ranges of stone and rock. With the trail
lost, you might never find your way out of the mountains.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
You wonder what force could have dug this alcove in the side of the mountains.
The only exit appears to be back to the north where you came from. However, this
alcove does seem to offer protection from the creatures of the mountains.
Perhaps this would be a good place to rest for a while.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
The trail is stronger here, giving you a sense of confidence. A slope to the
south appears to lead up to a mountain peak. However, your trail travels from
the north, continuing east. The eastward path, which flanks the steep, bare,
dangerous-looking flank of a mountain, goes up towards another peak in the Misty
Mountains.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
With the path sloping upwards, you are able to reach another peak of the
mountains to the east. To the north, you see a strong trail leading through the
mountains. All other directions are blocked by high walls of stone which slant
over you slightly, threatening to fall and crush you.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
From your perch on the top of this mountain, you can see that all but one side
is very steep. To the south, you can see that the same holds true for most of
the peaks in that direction. Far in the west, another row of mountain tops rises
in the air. As there is nothing of interest here, you decide that you should go
back the way you came, and continue your journey.
name: Mountain Peak

desc:
As you climb down, you lose your footing and fall. Although you land
comfortably, you find that you have no way of getting back up. Rising before
you, a mountain peak stands high in the clouds. Climbing shouldn't be too
difficult, and you quickly decide that standing here might not be very safe.
Perhaps you can find a way down the other side of the mountain top.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
After a tough climb, you finally manage to reach the peak of this mountain. All
around you, ageless snow rests. There is some fresh snow sifted on the very top
of the snow cap. From this height, you can see that a long chain of mountains
are strung from the north to south. Looking back down, you realise that your
climb up must have dislodged some of the mountainside, as returning below is now
impossible.
name: Atop a Peak

desc:
As you follow the trail, the passage to the west is suddenly filled with large
boulders! Luckily, you dodge the falling rocks. Unfortunately, passage that way
is now hopelessly blocked. However, the trail appears to continue high up to a
snow capped peak. Although it's a steep climb, you should be able to manage it
without too much problem.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
As with the slope leading up here, the wind is stronger then you'd like.
However, from this height, the threat of falling is much greater. You decide you
should make haste and leave from this mountain top as soon as you can. Before
you leave, however, you notice that the peak just south of you rises higher than
most of the mountains in this area. Curiosity drives you to explore it further.
name: Mountain Peak

desc:
The wind in this region of the mountains is much stronger than elsewhere.
Whipping about you, it desperately tries to knock you off balance. Just south of
you, you see a trail that travels through the mountains. Perhaps it leads to
refuge of some sort. As well, you can climb to a mountain peak from here.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
You carefully pick your way between the rocks, knowing that one small slip could
mean death. Looking up you can see the majestic peak of this mountain with the
line of peaks snaking away to the south east like a worm glistening in the sun.
name: On the Mountainside

desc:
You find that the trail runs through a valley at this point. You are able to
make your way along the trail north and south. As well, you can follow a slope
up towards the west. A large pile of rocks and boulders block any chance of
passing to east.
name: Narrow Trail

desc:
Here, the trail becomes faint and appears to continue in several directions. You
can follow it up towards a mountain top, or east and south deeper into the
mountains. If only you had a map of the region, things might be easier.
Unfortunately, not even a map would help much, as by some evil magic, the true
path seems to change occasionally. Or perhaps that's just a false rumour spread
by those creatures who would rather have you as a dinner guest...
name: Trail through the Mountains

desc:
A rather smooth trail begins here and heads off towards a ridge in the east. As
well, you can follow a smaller path up to the peak of a mountain. Southeast of
here, an extremely tall mountain rises high into the clouds. Perhaps if you
could find your way to that peak, you could see the region better, and find your
way out of here.
name: Trail through the Mountains

desc:
From this peak, you have a clear view of a much higher peak rising to the south.
The way to it, however, seems to be complicated. A trail continues east from
here, as well as going down from the peak, and west. In the distance, you see a
chain of more peaks in the west. To the north, the long chains all seem to come
together.
name: Trail beside a Peak

desc:
You are walking on a trail leading up the side of a mountain. A vast range of
mountains spreads out to the west and south, with a magnificent line of peaks
weaving down towards the south-east. To the east the path you are on leads
straight as an arrow towards a tall pillar of rock, and to the north lies a
dense forest, but no obvious way of getting there.
name: Trail Leading Upwards

desc:
A large sheet of rock from the south overhangs this area. As you stare at this
overhang, your mind drifts into thoughts of its creation at the hands of the
ancients. A picture comes into your mind of the massive upheaval Arda suffered
when Morgoth was finally cast out. Even today, many ages later, signs of that
battle remain throughout the lands.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
The thick dust of the mountains makes your way difficult. Both to your north and
south, the way is impassable. However, it appears that you can climb to yet
another peak of these mountains from here. Perhaps from there you can find your
destination, and a way to get there...
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
A long chain of mountain peaks are strung along the north and south. However,
because of the gaps between them, none are accessible from here. From your
perch, you notice two mountains with snow-capped peaks. One is south of here,
while the other is somewhat east.
name: Mountain Peak

desc:
You are standing at the bottom of a huge mountain. Far above you, some small,
dark dots can be seen next to the white peak of the mountain. Probably birds
flying high in the sky. North of you, the trail is leading west-east, through
the snowy and cold Misty Mountains. It is probably possible to try to climb all
the way up, though it seems to be harder the further you get, as the slope gets
steeper.
name: Dangerous Climb

desc:
You are still on the trail, wondering where it will take you. You know that many
of these trails far into the Mountains have bad ends. Still, you feel that you
are on the track of the right way through these mountains. Walk on!
name: Trail

desc:
This may well be the High Pass, the most important trade route across this part
of the Mountains. Few travel across now, however. Orcs lurk in this area, making
all journeys very dangerous. Sometimes, you spot orkish corpses and other signs
of combat.
name: Trail

desc:
The trail makes a few bends. You expect it to end behind every corner, but it
goes on every time. You wonder if you soon reach the opposite side of these
mountains. You have been walking quite some time now, but still you only see
tall mountain-peaks in every direction.
name: Trail

desc:
This trail takes a sharp turn to the south to avoid a high ridge, and then
continues its steady climb toward a high pass to the west. Eastward, it
continues its tortuous path - winding among the peaks and valleys of the Misty
Mountains.
name: Trail

desc:
The narrow trail makes a sharp bend from west to north here. To the west, it
climbs steadily toward a high pass. The trail is in bad shape - frequently it is
almost blocked by fallen rocks and small landslides. The high peaks that
surround the area gleam with pristine white snow.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
You walk on a trail, well, whether it is a 'trail' or just a heap of stones is
surely a matter suited for discussion. Anyway, you make a stop here. To the
southeast, the trail is flooded. A stream flows rapidly downwards, and it will
soon take that part of the trail down to the bottom of the valley north of you.
name: Trail

desc:
Well, you guess this can be called a trail. You have seen better ones, but it is
obvious that someone has been here before you. The trail goes east-west. The
mountain tops are far above you. The wind at the tops is strong: You see clouds
of snow being formed up there.
name: Trail

desc:
The trail runs north-south here, and the ground on both sides is relatively flat
in comparison to the rest of the area. Further to the north, you can see the
mountains rising up, and you can sometimes catch a glimpse of a rather large
bird in the distance.
name: Angmarian Trail

desc:
Standing on top of one of the highest peaks in this range, you are overwhelmed
by the power of the sight that greets you. In the north you catch a glimpse of a
path leading east to west, and beyond that a dense forest. To the south east
this line of peaks leads your eyes in a dazzling sinuous dance.
name: On Top of a Mountain

desc:
The ground underfoot is somewhat rocky, but much easier going than travelling
through the mountains. Obviously it was built by someone or something walking
this way many times, for it is both relatively worn and wide. To the east the
path seems to lead towards a pillar of rock, while several miles to the west the
path starts to lead up the side of a mountain.
name: Trail through the Mountains

desc:
Standing in the middle of this rocky trail, the magnificence of the surrounding
mountains is obvious for anyone who would care to look. The wandering line of
mountain-tops leads your eyes in a mesmerising dance, light glistening off the
caps like sun on wet skin. The trail you are on leads west and east.
name: Trail through the Mountains

desc:
Leading east and west, this trail seems to meet another at some short distance
in the east. In the east there appears to be a large pillar of black stone,
standing out in sharp contrast to the whiteness of the snow-clad mountains
behind.
name: Trail through the Mountains

desc:
Directly in the east the path you are on intersects another leading north and
south. In the east stands a tall pillar of stone, contrasting with the snow-
clad mountain tops behind it. The shape of the pillar is not obvious from this
far away, but it definitely seems to have been carved, either by hand or by the
weather. To the west the path leads up a mountain in the distance.
name: Trail through the Mountains

desc:
This is the intersection of the well travelled path leading east-west, and an
older but less used path leading north-south. The mountainside slopes steeply
downwards in the north, and rises slightly to the south. In the east stands a
black pillar which has obviously been shaped by something, but its precise form
cannot be told from this distance.
name: Crossing of the Paths

desc:
In the east the path seems to make a bend to the south, veering around a large,
imposing pillar of black rock. In the south the cloud-wreathed tops of the
mountains rear high, while in the west the path leads up the side of a steep
mountain.
name: Trail through the Mountains

desc:
The path leads north, south and east here. In the west the sheer size of the
mountains rising above you makes you feel very inadequate, but the sight of the
homely hut in the east cheers you up, with the small wisp of smoke curling from
the chimney.
name: Rocky Path

desc:
The trail bends north and west here, around a small outcrop of rock. To the
west, a mountain peak looms high above your head, but you can see no way of
climbing up. In the east the mountains dip down to a valley before rising again
to form some sightly less formidable hills.
name: Trail on a Mountainside

desc:
This rough trail leads south along a narrow ledge on the side of a mountain. The
way both up and down is too steep for you to attempt to climb, so you must
either follow this trail south, or go north onto the path leading east and west.
name: Trail on a Mountainside

desc:
The path is rocky, but not so rocky as to make it hard to walk. In the south you
can hear the gurgling of water from a spring, in the east you see a small wooden
hut, and to the west you see the large range of Misty Mountains rising far above
your head.
name: Rocky Path

desc:
The water bubbles merrily from a crack in the rocks and flows into a little
pond. There must be an underground outlet for the water, because the pool never
seems to get any fuller, yet the water doesn't leave here in any observable
manner. This looks like a good place to rest your weary bones, or maybe the hut
you can see in the east would be more comfortable?
name: Bubbling Spring

desc:
The rocks underfoot are rather loose, but thankfully you managed to find a
relatively compact patch on which to walk with safety. From here you can climb
the mountain to the west, or traverse north along the side of the mountain.
name: On the Mountainside

desc:
The trail is almost at an end here, dropping down into a valley which leads
south-east. You can see mountains in the east and west, although those in the
east don't seem to be so formidable.
name: The Trail

desc:
The trail bends south and east here, dipping down to a valley bottom in the
south, leading along the side of a mountain in the east. To the west the peaks
of this mountain chain rise majestically to their full height, glistening in the
sun.
name: Trail on a Mountainside

desc:
Standing on the side of this mountain makes you feel slightly uneasy, since you
realise that you could possibly lose your footing at any time and fall down the
slope. To the west you can climb further up this magnificent mountain, or you
can traverse the mountainside to the south.
name: On the Mountainside

desc:
Standing on the top of this mountain gives you a commanding view of the area
around you. This line of peaks weaves sinuously down to the south east, In the
north a path leads east-west, and further north you can see a dense forest.
name: On Top of a Mountain

desc:
The snow crunches underfoot as you travel precariously along a narrow ridge. The
way down the west side of this mountain looks impossible, but in the apex of
this peak and the one further east, you may be able to descend safely to the
valley below.
name: On Top of a Mountain

desc:
You are walking on a small trail, leading around the mountain. This trail is
probably frequently used by travellers since it is well marked and easy to walk
upon. Here, you will have to climb up to continue your way around the mountains.
You can, however, spot a small trail leading between the mountains further west.
name: Trail around the Mountain

desc:
You are walking on a small, but well-trodden path between the mountains. This
path seems to have been an important fast connection between the east and west
of this huge range of mountains. You are completely surrounded by mountains on
all sides of you.
name: Path between the Mountains

desc:
You have left the lowlands behind you, and you notice the mountains which seemed
far away, have indeed grown in size. You can see no trees here, only some small
crooked bushes. A couple of crows are flying high up in the sky.
name: Path to the Mountains

desc:
This path is slowly ascending, making its way to the mountains you can see to
the north. You can indeed see few trees here, making the landscape pretty
desolate. The path continues to the east, or you can go down to the lowlands
west of you.
name: Path to the Mountains

desc:
Lots of stones are lying here which make walking difficult. The mountains that
seemed so small from afar have now risen and nearly cover your view in all
directions. To the north you can enter a pebbled path, or you can follow this
path east or west.
name: Sloping Ground

desc:
The landscape changes as you move. There are big mountains in front of you with
snow on their peaks. The only way to get further east is by passing through
them. You can see a big cliff in the distance to the north. The main trail goes
east-west.
name: A Trail

desc:
You have come to a small crossing of paths. To the north you barely notice an
old twisted path. As with other paths in this area, it seems to lead nowhere.
You hear howls in the distance, dangerous creatures might be near. The main path
still goes east-west.
name: Small Crossing

desc:
You notice the land looks even wilder here. You can see mountains on all sides
of you, and you begin to wonder what might live up here so far from
civilisation. The vegetation is sparse, only a few crooked low trees around. The
wind is cold and chilling, coming from the mountains. However, the path
continues to the east, or you can turn back westwards.
name: Trail

desc:
You make your way through a heap of stones, a result of an avalanche bringing
lots of loose rocks with it. The side of the mountain is covered by small
birches, except the area where the avalanche once flattened everything in its
path. Once across the heap of stone, you see that the trail goes on.
name: Trail

desc:
Still walking on the trail, you study geological formations of this area. The
mountains are grey, clad with scattered spots of snow. Snow tends to lie in
areas well sheltered from wind. Sometimes, during a long winter, much snow is
gathered, that it will stay throughout the summer. In areas also well hidden
from the sun, large glaciers have formed. The path runs west to east, while a
seemingly less used trail splits off to the south.
name: Trail

desc:
This might well be the famous High Pass. This part seems well trodden, although
it may have been a while since people last wandered this route. You believe that
a pile of stones caused by an avalanche or rock slide has been recently removed
from the trail. It is said that Beornings from the Anduin valley east of these
mountains keep the Pass open. You really hope that it was Beornings, not Orcs,
that cleared the trail. You walk around a sharp bend in the trail and find it
blocked by a large rock slide. No one has bothered to remove that pile, or the
the rocks have slided down recently. You should be able climb to the other side,
but you doubt you will be able to go the same way back.
name: Trail

desc:
This narrow and seldom used path winds its way through the passes of the
Hithaeglir. Despite the frequent switchbacks and bends, you think that the trail
is following on the average a north-south course. The surroundings are very cold
and rocky, not even a blade of grass can grow here. Just after a bend, your way
is blocked by a huge pile of boulders. You can only retrace your steps.
name: Trail through the Mountains

desc:
You have fallen down a long, long slope. The snow that made the fall so slippery
cushioned you from any damage, although getting back up may be a pain. The air
in this room is very cold, probably due to the vast amounts of snow around you.
name: A Deep Valley

desc:
This path winds treacherously along the side of the mountain. To the north the
mountainside descends very steeply to the valley below. A cave mouth yawns
darkly in the east. Maybe this could offer you some degree of shelter from the
elements.
name: On the Side of a Mountain

desc:
The path hugs the contours of the mountain. The mountain rises steeply to the
south, and falls down to a deep valley to the north. The path leads east and
west. Directly west you catch a glint of reflected light.
name: Perilous Path

desc:
The noise here is deafening, you can hardly hear yourself think. The water is
crashing down from above, collecting in a large pool, and flowing over the
northern face of this mountain, to crash on another ledge farther below. Spray
dances all around, reflecting the light in many colours, and soaking your
clothes. Looking carefully you can see a path leading up the rocks on the south
side of the waterfall, but it looks dangerous and you may not be able to climb
back down this way.
name: Bottom of the Waterfall

desc:
That last move was a mistake. Even though the handhold looked secure, the
condensed water on its surface made it very slippery, so you lost your grip and
fell. I think maybe you should listen to advice in future. Being adventurous is
all very well, but that should be tempered with caution.
name: Under the Waterfall

desc:
The water runs into here from the West, then cascades over the edge of the rock
ledge on which you are standing. Clouds of spray billow up from below and cast
reflections all around. The water collects in tear-shaped droplets on your
clothes, your ears..in fact, they collect on any surface. Climbing back down
that steep path which you climbed up is probably not a good idea..the mist
seriously reduces your vision, and makes the rock very slippery.
name: Above a Waterfall

desc:
Clear sparkling water runs cheerily from a crack in the rocks here. More than
likely this water is a product of melting snows high on the mountain tops, and
it is refreshingly cooling after your long climb. Gurgling happily the water
flows to the east. In the south a short tunnel crosses one of the mountains, it
seems to lead towards the main path.
name: A Sparkling Spring

desc:
This short tunnel is lit by light coming from both ends and is only about twenty
feet in length. High enough for the tallest elf to walk through unhindered, it
must have been built by some pretty large creatures. The walls are smooth,
although they show some marks of cutting tools where the passage was hacked out
of the mountainside.
name: Tunnel through a Mountain

desc:
Here, south of the trail, few living things can be seen. The trees and plants
that has survived are crooked and miserable. All around you, mountains are
reaching for the sky and a cold wind blows through the valleys and over the
peaks. Some tracks of something or someone can be seen, and some remains of
animals are scattered across the landscape. A descending trail leads to the
east, while north of you, the trail is leading upwards.
name: South of the Trail

desc:
You are walking carefully on a stony path. Here you can see stones, boulders and
pebbles of every conceivable size and shape. The path is difficult to walk upon.
To the west you see a large rising mountain, and you notice the snowy peaks
through the haze. You can continue on the path and move further south, or you
can return north.
name: Pebbled Path

desc:
You are walking on a stony path. Here you can see stones, boulder and pebbles of
every conceivable size and shape. The path is difficult to walk upon. Here, the
path splits east-west.
name: Pebbled Path

desc:
You are walking carefully on a stony path. Here you can see stones, boulders and
pebbles of every conceivable size and shape. The path is difficult to walk upon.
The path continues to the north, or you can go back west.
name: Pebbled Path

desc:
You are walking on a stony path. Here you can see stones, boulders and pebbles
of every conceivable size and shape. The path is difficult to walk upon. You see
a cliff to the north of you.
name: Pebbled Path

desc:
You are walking among mountains on both sides of you. The path seems to be
well-trodden though, and you can move pretty fast here. You hear no birds here,
or anything else. A cold wind makes you feel cold all over. The path leads north
and west.
name: Path among the Mountains

desc:
You are walking among mountains, with peaks on both sides of you. On your left
side you see a big mountain, which you can try to climb up the side of. This
path seems to be well-trodden though, and you can move pretty fast here. You
hear no birds singing, only the echo of your footsteps. You can either stay on
the path going south, or you can try to climb up on the top of the mountain.
name: Path among the Mountains

desc:
At this crossing, several paths meet. One trail seems to lead west, another one
leads south, and one can go down here. This place seems like a meeting ground of
some sort. A ring of large stones in the middle is likely acting as a waymark.
name: Way-meet in the Mountains

desc:
You make your way on a small rugged track in the mountains. The trail is slow to
walk upon, because there are many stones and pebbles to move around all the
time. You face a big mountaintop to the south of you, and the trail turns east
here.
name: Trail in the Mountains

desc:
You make your way on a small rugged track in the mountains. Stones and pebbles
are scattered all over the path, making it hard to walk upon. The vegetation is
scarse, with only a few trees and bushes growing beside the road. You are
surrounded by mountaintops, and the air is chilly.
name: Trail in the Mountains

desc:
This is the end of the misty path. It is still hard to see anything here, due to
all the mist that covers this place. You can either go north, or try to climb
upwards, though something seems to be in your way if you want to go up.
name: Misty Path

desc:
You are walking on a very misty path. It is hard to see anything here, due to
the mist that covers this place. The mist seems to naturally be hanging over
this place all the time. It is hard to see anything clearly here, but you notice
that this path seems to be the correct way up to the mountains.
name: Misty Path

desc:
You make your way on a small rugged track in the mountains. Lots of stones and
pebbles seems to be in your way all the time, making the path slow to walk upon.
You see some mountain tops to the south, and a huge stone in the middle of the
road catches your eye.
name: Trail in the Mountains

desc:
You are walking in the mountains, without following any special path. It is easy
to walk here. You can barely spot the top of the mountain, partly hidden in
clouds. Maybe it is possible to make your way all the way up to the top. There
seems to be a trail to the south of you.
name: On a Mountain

desc:
You have come to a very sharp turn in the path. You notice that this path must
lead up to the mountains, as you can see more mountains around you. It's
difficult to walk here, due to all the small pebbles and rocks everywhere. You
can leave south and head for the mountains, or you can go east into a narrow
pass.
name: Sharp Bend

desc:
The trees here are blocking out the sun completely, making it hard for you to
see anything clearly here. At the western part of the valley, a huge boulder
seem to be covering some kind of an entrance. No birdsong can be heard here, and
the stems of the trees seem to be waiting for something or someone.
name: Gloomy Valley

desc:
It is hard to see anything in here, but you notice very fast that this is a
troll lair, where the trolls multiply to roam across the Misty Mountains. You
can spot a huge table in the middle of the room, and you also see heaps of
broken bones lying all over the earthfloor.
name: Troll Lair

desc:
You are at the end of this dirty cave. There is a big shelf on the wall, which
probably serves as food storage. You can see some corpses of unknown races lying
there, skulls cracked and bones broken. No weak creature could have done that.
name: Back Of Cave

desc:
You are walking on a stony path. Here you see stones, boulders and pebbles of
every conceivable size and shape which makes walking difficult on the path. You
can hear the sound of babbling water to the north. A steep slope leads to the
west, or you can continue north towards the sound of water.
name: Pebbled Path

desc:
At last, you are standing on this old bridge. It looks like it was built long
ago, but still spans the swift river to help travellers get across. You can
actually enter the river itself down here, but you need a boat or some kind of
floating device. To the north, there is a tall mountain rising up before you,
and to the south you can see a pebbled path.
name: A Creaking Bridge

desc:
You are standing at the foot of a large mountain. It didn't look as large from a
distance. The sound of babbling water can be heard from the south. A stony trail
edged with brown grass leads up the mountain. You can either climb up, or go
south toward the sound of water.
name: Foot of a Mountain

desc:
You are walking in the mountains. It is very easy to walk here. One problem with
staying here is that you can be easily seen. You also take the risk of falling
down into a gorge or being trapped by foul creatures that may still roam the
land. This seems to be a dead end. You can not go any further south by walking.
Your only exit is to the north.
name: On a Mountain

desc:
Finally you made your way up here. It wasn't an easy climb at all, and you
notice how cold and chilling the wind is here. There is a great view westwards,
you can see mountain peaks and other heights far away, with snow gleaming on the
tops.
name: On a Mountain

desc:
You are walking in the mountain, without following any special path. It is easy
to walk here. You have arrived at the end of this mountain. To the north, you
face many more mountains. They look sharp and dreadful.
name: On a Mountain

desc:
A huge solid rock protrudes out of the mountain, sheltering this place from cold
stinging winds and the blistering sun. It extends precariously out over this
sheltered platform, seemingly ready to crash down on it at moments notice, at a
whisper of eastern wind. The only way from here is the climb up the mountain in
that same direction.
name: Under a Hanging Rock

desc:
You make your way to the top of the hill. From here, you can see the water
pouring down the side of the mountain. The air here is fresh, and you can hear
the sound of rushing water. To the north, you can walk upon a ridge, or you can
go back down to your east.
name: On a Steep Slope

desc:
You find yourself at the bottom of a big cliff. At first, this appears to be a
dead end. There are many big rocks here and it seems possible to climb up the
side of the cliff. You may, however, leave this place and go back to the path to
the south.
name: Bottom of a Cliff

desc:
You are standing at the end of the ridge. Below, you can see an old bridge which
looks rather primitive. Water from a gap in the mountain flows to make a
waterfall. The melted snow from the mountain peaks flows toward the river bed
adding strength to the rushing water. The only exit is to the south.
name: On the Ridge

desc:
You struggle to climb your way up the side of the cliff. You can see far in all
directions. To the east, you see big mountains, and southward you see rocky
plains and even some small trees. There is a ridge to the north of you. However,
you notice more cliffs to the west if you decide to go that way, or you can go
down again.
name: On a Cliff

desc:
Ooooooooooops! Oh Dear! You did a big mistake here. You slip and fall and your
body is smashed to pieces at the bottom of the valley. You wonder if anyone will
ever find your corpse here as your life fades away... .
name: A Valley FAR down

desc:
This is the end of the ridge. From here, you have a great view in all four
directions, but you see mostly mountains everywhere. Below you, a swift mountain
stream is pouring out of a gap in the mountain. An extremely steep path is
sloping down to the shore. It looks very dangerous!
name: On the Edge

desc:
This is a narrow ridge. You see the ground far below you on both sides, and you
have a great view over the surrounding area. A couple of birds can be seen in
the sky and a few small trees exist here. The edge of this ridge is to the
north.
name: On a Ridge

desc:
You walk upon the edge of the cliff, which seems to end here. The only way to
continue is to climb down to the bottom of the cliff. It looks like a small dark
valley down there, and seems like an ideal place for creatures that don't like
light. You can go back east, or climb down.
name: On a Cliff

desc:
You find yourself north of a cliff. There seems to be a small path leading up
towards it. Pebbles and rocks are scattered in the brown grass beside the path.
A few living plants can also be seen. The wind coming from the north is chilly
and freezing and there is no shelter in sight. A couple of birds can be seen
high in the sky.
name: Below a Ledge

desc:
You are floating in the middle of a mountain stream. The water flows rapidly and
you can clearly see the water pouring out of the big gap on the side of the
mountain east of you. It seems impossible to move in that direction. You can
leave up back to the bridge, or you can stay in the water of the stream which is
moving west.
name: Mountain River

desc:
You are floating in the middle of a mountain stream. The water seems to be more
calm here than to the east. On both sides of you, you can see rocky walls,
making it impossible to climb up on any side. The river goes east-west.
name: Mountain Stream

desc:
You are floating in the middle of a mountain stream. The water is dark and
ominous, but calm. The rocky walls seem to bend over trying to reach you. The
river makes a sharp bend to the south here.
name: Mountain Stream

desc:
You are floating in the middle of the mountain stream. On both sides of you,
there are big rocky walls which make it impossible for you to enter on any
shore. The river flows west, but it is possible to go back north.
name: Mountain Stream

desc:
Here the river goes underground. The swiftly running water makes it difficult
for you to control your craft. You notice a landing to the north. You can go
north or back east.
name: Gap in the Mountain

desc:
This landing is surrounded by rocky walls and cliffs. On all sides of you, there
are rocks and stones of all shapes and sizes. A dangerous looking hut lies to
the east, its walls rotten and murky. A very small path leads to it. To the
south, you can see the mountain river you came from.
name: A Landing

desc:
You enter an old hut. It has been a very long time since it was last used by the
local mountaineers. The hut has no windows or other exits except from the broken
door that you entered. It is a very primitive hut, but still servicable for use
as shelter for the night from the cold winds.
name: An Old Mountain Hut

desc:
You made your way to the top, and do not regret the climbing at all. You can see
far away in all directions here. Unfortunately, the very cold wind and thin air
up here, make you easy exhausted, and you should better get down before you
catch a cold. You better take it easy when you move down again.
name: Top of a Mountain

desc:
It is hard to observe many details here, because it is very dark. The trees act
as roof preventing sunlight from entering. You hear the sound of water nearby,
and you see the water pouring out from a small crack in the side of the cliff. A
path leads to the south, but you can also go west to an even darker part of this
valley.
name: Dark Valley

desc:
It is really hard to see anything here. However, this place looks evil and the
air here is thick and heavy. The trees close out any light from above and no
birds are singing here. You seem to be at the end of this dark valley. The only
exit is to the east.
name: Dark Valley

desc:
This small cave enclosed in darkness doesn't give away many secrets. There are
piles of bones spread all over, and a couple of corpses give the cave a dank,
foetid smell. This is the home of some trolls. The only exit from here is to the
north.
name: Troll Cave

desc:
This appears to be just another dead end. On all sides of you there are big
stone walls which prevent you from travelling in any direction except back the
way you came. The trail leads to the southern wall where it abruptly ends at
some loose boulders.
name: End of Trail

desc:
The foetid smell of rotten corpses hits your senses as you enter this room. You
see broken bones lying in the corners. There is a northern wall where some large
rocks have been placed on each other to serve as a chair. This has to be the
home of a large creature. The only exit is to the south.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
You have finally reached the bottom of the slope. There, are many stones here
and there appears to have been some kind of water recently. Somehow the water
has drained away to other places or has simply evaporated. The stones are smooth
and dark green. You notice big footsteps in the soft soil. Something huge has
walked these desolate parts of Arda. To the north you see a rocky wall.
name: Dried Out Lake

desc:
As you move on, you notice that the trees grow larger here. You can see that
this path has not been used for a long time. It is overgrown with dry moss, and
rotten leaves spot the ground. The path descends gently to the west.
name: Old Path

desc:
Everywhere around you, the Misty Mountains cover your view. There seems to be no
end to these huge mountains with their white peaks. A small tree can be seen in
the middle of all those boulders and rocks, its small branches reaching no
further than a few feet out from the tiny, brown stem.
name: In the Mountains

desc:
Everywhere around you, the Misty Mountains cover your view. Boulders and rocks
make it hard for you to walk here and the vegetation is sparse. The wind is
howling and freezing to your bones. Once in a while, a bird can be seen in the
sky.
name: In the Mountains

desc:
The Misty Mountains seem invulnerable to the icy wind that blows here. It is
very hard to get used to it, whipping at you all the time. Rocks and pebbles are
lying all around, making travelling here tiresome. The mountains seem to never
end. Once you have climbed one, there is another one waiting for you on the
other side. South of you a huge rock can be seen.
name: North of a Huge Rock

desc:
Towering over other big boulders and rocks, you see this one huge rock, which is
the size of over ten metres across. Atop of it some bones can be seen, as if a
bird had been sitting there, chewing on some dead animal and then simply dropped
the remains. When you move closer to the huge rock, you see some small boulders
lying in a heap beside it, they seem to cover a hole in the ground beneath this
huge rock.
name: A Huge Rock

desc:
Between rocks and boulders there seems to be a small path, though it his hard to
find anything living around here who could possibly have made it. It is mostly a
tiny little path, where pebbles seem to have been kicked aside. Scattered beside
it, there are a few living plants and some grass, which seem more dead than
alive. To the west, you see a huge mountain rising towards the sky.
name: East of a Mountain

desc:
You find yourself north of a huge mountain, its snowy peak sparkling white. The
wind from the north is freezing your bones, making them ache. But north of you
there is a valley, it might suffice as shelter from the wind. Though it might be
hard to get back up here again, once you have climbed or fallen down. The
mountain prevents you from going south here, but there seems to be a way to
circle it, if you travel southwestish. Rocks and boulders are in your way
wherever you look, and it seems hard to find anything alive here.
name: North Side of a Mountain

desc:
South of you the snowy peak of a huge mountain rises towards the sky, where some
birds can be seen. It seems impossible to get to the top, but there is a way to
circle it and get to the southern side of the mountain. North of you, the slope
gets steeper and ends up in a dark valley. It seems like it would be hard to get
back up here, once you have climbed or fallen down. This is a very exposed
place, making the wind chilly and hard to find shelter from. Somehow, a few
plants have managed to survive, hiding between the boulders.
name: North Side of a Mountain

desc:
You are slowly climbing the mountain. You see no path anywhere, just a lot of
stones and rocks. It is hard to walk quickly here. The mountain side you are
climbing is pretty steep, sometimes when a rock comes loose, it does not stop
rolling down the side until it is out of view. Further up the mountain side, the
top can be seen, but there is no way to reach it. You can either travel down the
mountainside again, or try to circle around the top to the northern side.
name: Almost at the Top

desc:
North of you, a huge mountain rises towards the sky, its snow clad peak gleaming
bright white. A small trail is lingering its way up the mountain, between rocks
and boulders. Maybe it is possible to reach the top. South of you, the trail
leading west-east is twisting through the Misty Mountains. This area seems very
grey and it is hard to find anything alive here except grass and tiny plants.
name: South Side of a Mountain

desc:
You find youself at the side of a mountain. North of you, a snow clad peak rises
towards the sky. It blocks all your view north, but there seems to be a way to
get further up the mountain if you walk east. The ground is rocky, but between
the rocks, grass and other small plants have managed to survive. South of you,
the trail leading west-east is lingering its way through the Misty Mountains.
name: South Side of a Mountain

desc:
Boulders and rocks make it hard for you to travel here. A few plants live here
but those are not what first catches your eye. A huge mountain rises northwest
of you, its white peak gleaming bright. There seems to be a way towards the top
somewhere west of you. South of you, the trail leading west-east lingers its way
through the mountains, there seems to be a cliff hanging over it southeast of
you, though it might be hard to get there. It would probably give you a good
view of whoever might be travelling the trail.
name: South Side of a Mountain

desc:
You find yourself on a rock above a small path. The path is twisting through the
mountains and beneath you it goes through a small cleft. You have a good view of
the path in both directions, west and east, and you can see if anyone travels on
it. The rock you are standing on is not very stable and you had better watch
your step or you might fall down.
name: Rock above the Path

desc:
Beneath this huge rock, you find a deep rocky hole, which seems to be the home
of something or someone. Torn pieces of clothes, bones and other bodyparts are
scattered all across this hole. The temperature is higher than above, but the
air is very damp.
name: A Deep Rocky Hole

desc:
This area of dark pine forest lies at the eastern end of a long valley. In every
direction save west, high cliffs of granite loom overhead - their stark rock
surface adding a forbidding aspect to this lovely vale.
name: Deep Valley

desc:
This narrow valley is hidden deep in the towering peaks of the Misty Mountains,
kept isolated by the high cliffs that form its walls. The valley floor is home
to a dense forest of pine and cedar - so dense that only on the brightest of
days does an occasional beam of sunlight penetrate to the ground.
name: Deep Valley

desc:
You hesitate but do jump into the darkness, and you expect to die. In a few
seconds you will know...
name: Certain Death

desc:
The ground here is not entirely stabile, constantly giving off creaking and
cracking noises as the snow shifts. Travelling in any direction but back west
would be too perilous to consider.
name: On Some Snow

desc:
This forested valley provides a welcome respite from harsh mountains that
completely encircle it. The valley is deep enough that it is sheltered from the
frequent blizzards that sweep across the surrounding peaks in winter. To the
northwest there is a lake fed by several waterfalls that bounce down the
mountain's face.
name: Deep Valley

desc:
A long lake lies between the mountain peaks extending to the west. The calm
surface of the water reflects the towering mountains and the sky behind them.
Lumps of snow and ice float in the dark water like small islands. A majestic
mountain face rises from the water on the far northern shore of the lake, and to
the west this forest of pines and cedars continues. A tall spur of grey rock
blocks travel eastward.
name: Deep Valley

desc:
Tall pines and cedars tower overhead, completely blotting out all view of the
sky. To the north, a lake can be spied among the trees, probably fed by snowmelt
from the surrounding peaks. The valley continues both east and west, like a
verdant gash among the whites and greys of the Misty Mountains.
name: Deep Valley

desc:
This is the western end of an extremely deep valley. The slope to the west is
extremely steep, but it is possible to scramble up to the top of a ridge. The
northern portion of the valley holds a lake fed by many delicate water- falls
that dance down the cliff walls. To the east, the pines and cedars form a canopy
that keeps the forest floor shrouded in eternal twilight.
name: Deep Valley

desc:
The narrow trail continues - despite the damage caused by frequent rockfalls, it
is still fairly easy to follow. Down and to the east, there is a rather large
valley - probably formed by some ancient glacier in a long ago time.
name: Angmarian Trail

desc:
A long lake lies between the mountain peaks extending to the west. The calm
surface of the water reflects the towering mountains and the sky behind them.
Lumps of snow and ice float in the dark water like small islands. A majestic
mountain face rises from the water in the north, but to the south a narrow strip
of rocky ground extends between the lake and the mountain.
name: Pristine Lake

desc:
This lake is nestled in the bottom of a long valley that is completely
surrounded by tall peaks. Several ribbon-like waterfalls cascade down the cliffs
along the northern shore of the lake, while deep and gloomy forests fill the
remainder of the valley.
name: Pristine Lake

desc:
You walk on the surface of the glacier, and you have skilfully avoided falling
down into any cracks. You are pretty close to the top of the glacier, but a huge
crack prevents you from going further to the south. In a smaller crack you see
something strange. It is possible to walk down into it without risking your
neck.
name: Close to the Top

desc:
You are standing on an edge. In the shadow to the north lies a glacier. You can
enter a tunnel inside it through a small hole. Down to the south you see a long
steep slope filled with rocks and boulders. If you climb down, you won't be able
to climb back; it's too steep for that.
name: On the Edge

desc:
You find yourself at the bottom of a crack in the glacier. A pile of fallen
blocks of ice makes it possible to climb up on the surface of the glacier. Apart
from some blocks of ice, the ground is rocky here. A tunnel goes south and
north.
name: Bottom of a Crack

desc:
The tunnel goes west and south. There is a tunnel sloping upwards to the south.
You know that you will soon reach an exit if you continue in that direction.
name: Bend in the Tunnel

desc:
You are standing on the glacier and you look around you trying to spot any safe
routes that will take you across it or to the top. If you decide to move on
further across this glacier, you should watch your steps carefully! If you have
no experience in walking on surfaces like this, you better get off this glacier
right away.
name: On the Glacier

desc:
The trail stops here. The glacier blocks the way. You see the top of the glacier
to the south and you wonder if you can climb up there to get an overlook. To the
north you see the bottom part of the glacier, lying several hundreds of metres
below you. You look at the surface of the glacier and see that it is full of big
cracks and rocks.
name: At a Glacier

desc:
You have entered a tunnel inside the glacier. The tunnel must have been made of
water flowing through here. The ground is rocky and a bit wet, and the walls and
the roof have a deep blue colour. The temperature is higher in here than outside
and the wind isn't beating at you anymore. However, you can still see your own
breath.
name: Inside the Glacier

desc:
It is really gloomy here. Tall mountains block the sun throughout the whole
year. Nothing seems to live here, except some sparse bushes which are twisted
into miserable forms by the wind and the snow. As the trail bend southwards, you
see that it will take you to a glacier.
name: Bend around the Mountain

desc:
You follow a tunnel going slightly down. You think this tunnel will lead you
down to the bottom of the glacier. The world outside among the mountains of
Hitheaglir is horrifying, but inside this glacier you see great beauty. The icy
walls form various shades of a soft deep blue colour. The sound from the small
stream echoes all over through the tunnels.
name: A Tunnel through the Glacier

desc:
The tunnel bends west here, and you have to go on your knees to get further. The
floor of the tunnel is no longer rocky. The tunnel is almost cylindrical. You
better be careful! Ice is slippery stuff.
name: A Narrow Tunnel

desc:
Well, the tunnel didn't lead you down to the bottom of the glacier. Instead, the
tunnel continues down into a hole in the mountain.
name: Tunnel End

desc:
This narrow trail is well-hidden. It deliberately takes advantage of the rocky
terrain to remain almost invisible from the trail to the west. It rises at a
sharp angle, and the footing at times is treacherous. It seems to level out a
bit some distance to the east.
name: Steep Trail

desc:
The path ends here. You can't spot any safe passages up into the mountains from
here. Suddenly, you hear the sound of some falling rocks above you. Fortunately,
they stop before they reach the place you are standing. You had better leave
this place soon; you realise that a lot of huge boulders above you might fall
down any time. Rocks surrounding you and the steep mountainsides prevent you
from choosing any other safe way from this place except the one you came.
name: Trail End

desc:
You walk through a narrow pass. Actually, it seems to be a large crack, possibly
made by a great earthquake ages ago. This pass is a shortcut to the other side
of the mountain, but what awaits you there?
name: A Narrow Pass

desc:
You walk through a narrow pass. Actually, it seems to be a large crack, possibly
made by a great earthquake ages ago. This pass is a shortcut to the other side
of the mountain, but what awaits you there? There's a trail south of you.
name: A Narrow Pass

desc:
You choose to leave the trail. It's probably a bad idea; it's easy to get lost
if wandering around well off any road in these mountains. You better return to
the road while it's still within range of vision. A lot of snow covers the
mountains ahead.
name: Mountains

desc:
Where will this path take you? The path is very narrow here, and you have to
walk very carefully to avoid falling down into certain death! You doubt it was
very wise to follow this path.
name: Angmarian Trail

desc:
The icy wind chills you. You feel it blow through your clothes. You are still
walking on a trail bending its way through the Mountains. You look up the
mountain-sides, and realise that you would probably not escape an avalanche
right here. You better move on!
name: Angmarian Trail

desc:
The path goes higher up into the mountains to the west, and to the east you see
it going pretty straight forward, with a lot of small bends, though. You are
surrounded by tall mountains. You see long tails of clouds hanging from the
peaks as the wind is forced up the steep sides of the mountains, bringing much
snow as well.
name: Angmarian Trail

desc:
The trail head east-west here. Even though the trail is rocky and windy, there
has been enough traffic on it that there is a path worn down in it. When you
look to the north, you can see the mountains in the distance, and when the light
hits the top just right, you can see a bit of movement around the peak.
name: Angmarian Trail

desc:
The trail heads to the east and west here. There is a rocky valley to the north
which you might be able to reach safely. There is a large plateau to your west,
and off in the north you can see the higher peaks of the Misty Mountains. When
you look close, you can clearly see that there is some sort of bird flying about
the peaks.
name: Angmarian Trail

desc:
You are in an extremely rocky valley, and the sides of the mountains are far to
steep to climb up. You can't see very far, because no matter which direction you
turn, there is a boulder in your way. You should be careful, because anything
could be hiding behind the rocks.
name: Rocky Valley

desc:
You are in an extremely rocky valley, and the sides of the mountains are far to
steep to climb up. You can't see very far, because no matter which direction you
turn, there is a boulder in your way. You should be careful, because anything
could be hiding behind the rocks.
name: Rocky Valley

desc:
This valley has mountain walls so steep that climbing them would be
inconceivable. The boulders block the view in every direction. Cavernous
openings glare darkly from under the larger rocks, and one cannot be sure what
manner of a creature is hiding in them.
name: Rocky Valley

desc:
You are pretty sure that you are on the right track. Few seem to travel here
now, but this trail is far better than any other you have walked the last hours.
name: Angmarian Trail

desc:
The air is warmer down here, but the tall mountains make the place gloomy; the
sun never rises high enough to see it. The vegetation here is odd and quite
different from anything you have seen before. You wish to move on and get away
from these mountains. You can follow the path southwards through a narrow cleft,
or follow the path up the valley-side.
name: Angmarian Trail

desc:
You are in the lower part of this path going far up the mountain-side. In the
valley below you, there are trees and green low vegetation, unlike the flora up
in the mountains. It seems like a peaceful place, well hidden among the tall
peaks of the Mountains.
name: Angmarian Trail

desc:
A small path starts here. You can't get further up. You can follow the path down
to the bottom of the valley. The way down is very steep with many bends.
name: Angmarian Trail

desc:
Far below, at the foot of the ridge, a small trail snakes along the side of the
mountain. High up and to the west is a major peak crowned with snow. Although
the landscape is too severe to reach the trail directly, perhaps it might be
possible to descend at the gentler slopes to the south.
name: Ridge above a Trail

desc:
This steep slope is dotted with tough evergreens that have managed to get a
foothold among the rocks. At the foot of the incline there is some sort of trail
- whether it was made by animals or intelligent life is impossible to tell.
name: A Mountainside

desc:
This path seems to lead nowhere. From this point you have a good view of some
valleys leading in different directions south of you. You look for possible ways
further into the mountains, but you can't find any obvious ways. You are afraid
you will be lost in these unfriendly mountains if you continue further into
them, but it may also be that it's a short distance to the other side. You don't
know. Maybe you should try to find the path to Elrond's elven home, even though
it must be pretty far away.
name: Angmarian Trail

desc:
The trail is in a horrible state. There have been many avalanches here since
last time anybody did work on this trail. You doubt you can follow this trail
for long; it's hard to distinguish the trail from the rocky terrain around you.
name: Angmarian Trail

desc:
You walk on this path leading downwards. All the bends makes the way down to the
bottom quite long. There is a short and fast way down to the east of you, it is
very steep and looks dangerous. The path turns to the west and it is hard to see
where it is leading, since it is turning and twisting a lot. Stones, boulders
and mountains is all you see around you. Sometimes you can hear the sound of
someone running through the mountains... or maybe it is just a stone rolling
down a mountainside.
name: Angmarian Trail

desc:
This steep mountainside is hard to climb. Loose rocks and boulders makes it hard
to get a good grip anywhere. Low bushes are sometimes the only thing that keeps
you from falling down. Far above you a trail can be distinguished and below you
the mountain is flatter and safer. This is not a very good place to stay at.
name: A Steep Mountainside

desc:
You check the ground before you every step now, but you find that it is quite
safe to walk here. The trail is twisting down, but will it take you anywhere?
You are far away from the High Pass now. You have to find a way back, or else
you will be lost in these horrible mountains forever.
name: A Safe Trail

desc:
You are halfway down to the bottom of the valley. Walking on this path is
exhausting, no matter whether you climb down or up. You stop a short while to
let the wind dry off your sweat and then continue.
name: Angmarian Trail

desc:
This ridge runs from north to south, climbing steeply as it does. To the
southwest a high peak looms - this ridge leads to the mountain's shoulder. East
and west the steep slopes are too dangerous to attempt a descent - those wishing
to reach lower altitudes will have to follow the ridge north.
name: High Ridge

desc:
Snow and ice makes it difficult to go further. You don't see much up here
because of mist. You see, however, that the mist is even thicker higher up.
name: High on a Mountainside

desc:
This narrow arrete runs due south from here. To the north, it runs into what
appears to be a narrow trail. To the east and west, the slopes are extremely
steep so it is impossible to descend.
name: High Ridge

desc:
This is a path in the middle of the Misty Mountains. All around you are deep
valleys and tall mountain peaks. You wonder if this crooked path will take you
anywhere, but there is no use staying here. The path goes on to the north, maybe
towards Angmar, the old realm of the Witch-king. The path is blocked by a heap
of stones, however. You can't get any further.
name: Angmarian Trail

desc:
The mountainside has become a drab world of grey stone and white snow here above
the treeline. The only signs of life are occasional patches of lichen that eke
out a harsh existence. To the north, the steep slope continues, and it might be
possible to reach the summit.
name: On a Mountain

desc:
This rock shelf overlooks a dizzying panorama - a fall of several hundred feet
awaits to the south and east. Northward, it is possible to ascend the mountain,
for though the terrain is harsh, the slope is not as great as in other places.
name: An Eyrie

desc:
This area is actually somewhat level - a last bastion of flat terrain before the
final ascent to the mountain summit to the north. The ground is littered with
boulders, and patches of snow are present all year long.
name: High on a Mountain

desc:
Snow and ice are a permanent feature of this stark landscape. The air is thin
and hard to breathe, and all is eerily silent. The summit of the mountain lies
above, and it looks to be accessible by a difficult climb. Several hundred feet
below lies a somewhat flat area where it might be safe to rest.
name: Below the Summit

desc:
Here at the peak of the mountain the world is a silent landscape of snow and
ice. In every direction other mountains rear - some higher than this one -
hiding the horizon from view. The slope to the south is climbable, although far
from gentle, so that looks to be the only way to proceed.
name: Mountain Summit

desc:
A A A A A A A A A A A A A A A A A A A A A A H H H H *splat* You have just come
to an abrupt ending.
name: Abrupt Ending

desc:
The trail turns to the west and south here, providing you with an excellent view
of the mountains. The hills surrounding you are relatively flat, but there are
so many rocks and boulders in the way, that you are afraid you would get lost in
there.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
The hills here are not steep, but there are boulders everywhere, making you
watch your every step. You thought that you were just next to the trail, but as
you look around, you are not sure where you are anymore.
name: Rocky Slope

desc:
The hills here are not steep, but there are boulders everywhere, making you
watch your every step. You thought that you were just next to the trail, but as
you look around, you are not sure where you are anymore.
name: Rocky Slope

desc:
The hills here are not steep, but there are boulders everywhere, making you
watch your every step. You can finally see the trail to your west....for a while
you thought that it had just simply disappeared.
name: Rocky Slope

desc:
The hills here are not steep, but there are boulders everywhere, making you
watch your every step. You thought that you were just next to the trail, but as
you look around, you are not sure where you are anymore.
name: Rocky Slope

desc:
You risk your life climbing in these mountains. The mountain sides that are not
covered with snow, are very rocky. You must walk very carefully, or soon you
will be sliding down the mountainside among tons of loose rocks. South of you,
you see a trail going east-west.
name: Dangerous Mountains

desc:
You have reached the top of one of the climbable mountains. It is not one of the
tallest, but you have a fair view. You spot two possible trails through the
Mountains; one on the southern side of this mountain, and one down on the
northern side of this mountain.
name: Mountain Top

desc:
This steep channel leads you down to a deep gully below, or back up to that
magnificent but dangerous line of peaks. East, you spot a dark opening in the
rock, and you wonder what sort of beast might be living in such a hostile
environment.
name: On a Mountainside

desc:
Strange claw marks on the walls and scattered bones on the floor may give you
some idea that whatever lives here is not friendly. But looking at the seemingly
random scratches, you notice that they may actually be a crude form of writing.
name: A Shallow Cave

desc:
Rocks and debris lie strewn about the floor of this gully. The sides have been
worn smooth with the passing of water from many winter storms, but no water is
in evidence at the moment. The gully leads south, where it makes a bend to the
east, or you can climb back up a narrow channel in the mountainside.
name: In a Deep Gully

desc:
Here the gully bends to the east and north. You can spot no way to climb up the
mountainside from here, and so you must follow the crude path along the bottom
of this depression to wherever it may take you.
name: In a Deep Gully

desc:
The ditch bends to the west and south here, and to the south you spot a large
pile of rocks seeming to block off any further progress. To the west you can see
that the gully makes another bend to the north again. You can see no way of
climbing the mountain from here, so for better or worse you must follow the path
you are on.
name: In a Deep Gully

desc:
The floor is littered with refuse giving off a vile smell, and you would prefer
not to know what you were standing in. Strange snuffling noises come from the
north, behind the sturdy door, making you wonder what sort of animal is kept in
here.
name: Filthy Cave

desc:
Rotten straw is all the evidence of bedding in this cell, and a small channel
running down the side of the room is obviously the sanitation. The smell in here
is even worse than the smell outside, making you want to throw up.
name: Prison Cell

desc:
This cave looks like it was a full scale exercise in depravity. Filth has been
smeared over the walls, human remains lie in grotesque positions strewn all
about the floor. A strange scratching noise can be heard coming from the east.
name: Filthy Cave

desc:
Broken bones crunch loudly under your feet, and other things squish softly when
you stand on them. Remains of many dead beings litter the floor, and it is most
probably these remains which give rise to the awful stench which makes you want
to take out your weapon and slice off your nose.
name: Filthy Cave

desc:
This chamber has a high stone ceiling, and is longer than it is wide, almost
like a tunnel. The high walls are covered in minute cracks. The floor is covered
with dust and debris, but it's origin must remain unknown, since most of it has
decayed beyond recognition. To the south a natural archway leads into a smaller
chamber.
name: Inside the Mountain

desc:
As you pick your way carefully through the various sizes of rocks strewn about
the floor of the gully, you spot a pile of rocks to the south, but there is
something rather suspicious about the way they are sitting, you feel that there
is something not entirely natural about them. You can travel north or south.
name: In a Deep Gully

desc:
The path slopes downward quickly into a large pit filled with loose rocks and
gravel. Smoke rises from a tunnel in the mountainside, only to be dispersed by
the cool mountain air. Broken tools and scraps of cloth litter the area, along
with some bones.
name: A Slag Pit

desc:
The trail goes through a narrow cleft. You look at some rocks positioned pretty
close to the edges above you. You walk swiftly, but with great stealth through
the cleft, and no rocks fall down on your head. To the east, the trail is
blocked by a huge rock slide. You can't spot any obvious ways to get across it.
name: Trail

desc:
As you start walking up the mountainside, you notice the path that you left
north of you is getting smaller and smaller. And as you get higher up on the
slope, the view over the surroundings grows vaster. Stones and small bushes are
making it hard for you to walk here, but there is still a long way to climb if
you want to reach the top.
name: Dangerous Climb

desc:
At this height the wind seems to never stop blowing, chilly and freezing. There
is nowhere to hide from it. A little bit above you, you can see the snow
covering the peak, but it will not be easy to get there and definitaly not safe.
Walking back down is the other alternative, but stones are slippy and you might
fall.
name: Dangerous Climb

desc:
You have finally reached the top! It took a lot of effort, but now you are here.
The view over the Misty Mountains is fantastic, it is possible to see miles
away. Far west of you the land is flat and green but northeast the mountains is
growing higher and higher. You are standing on a glacier, which probably doesn't
even melt in the summer. There might be a small risk that huge parts of it falls
down the mountainside, and brings you with it. Anyway, the other side seems even
steeper, you better try to climb down the way you came.
name: At the Top

desc:
You find yourself standing on the edge of a mountain plateau. This must be the
end of the plateau, as you can see a path leading eastwards among all the
numerous mountains around here. To the south is a steep downward slope, all
sheer rock interlaced with ice-filled crevices.
name: Mountain Plateau
